text:
There was a little red bird that couldn't stop singing.

It sang all morning long as if to say, "Look at me! I'm the loudest bird in all the land!"

Later the other birds talked about how annoying the little pipper was, but really all they'd do is chortle.

By dusk, the little red bird could hardly sing anymore. It flopped down.

"I really did my best," it croaked softly.

That night, a big owl heard it all. So the next morning, the owl hooted a duet, blending harmony instead of harshness. And all the birds came to listen!

That's when the red bird realized it could soar with big birds like the wise owl.

Now, when it sings, it sings with wisdom. Who knew?

So remember the little red bird?

Go talk to someone nice.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
